behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so summaries can attribute statements to attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real STT model output quality (speaker diarization/labeling), so mocked STT would hide real failures (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). System-level HTTP realism is not sufficient because the core risk is model labeling correctness. Rejected Workflow: behavior text is specifically about STT label preservation, not multi-agent decision chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production STT API, then verify transcript speaker labels are present and consistently mapped well enough for downstream attendee attribution checks.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser/transform logic on transcript text and schema mapping within a local code boundary (MOCK-MISS says no required module boundary in the behavior itself). No real OS/network/subprocess or real model judgment is required. Rejected Integration: behavior text says "Transcript parsing extracts..." and does not require cross-module runtime effects to surface the bug.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript snippets into the parsing function and assert extracted assignment objects contain correct assignee and task text, including edge phrasing and multiple assignments.

behaviors.description: Live summary contains at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This checks end output quality across sequential agentic stages (meeting content -> transcription/assignment evidence -> LLM summary/action items). Multiple model-dependent handoffs can fail even when each wrapper passes in isolation (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP). Rejected Agentic: behavior text targets "live summary output" for assigned attendees during the meeting, implying full chained pipeline behavior rather than a single model call.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real meeting recording with known per-attendee task assignments, run the live pipeline, and assert each attendee with an assignment appears with at least one action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` schema section, not narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is deterministic routing/mapping logic once decisions are already identified. No real infrastructure or model output quality is needed. Rejected Integration: behavior text explicitly says "routes identified decisions," which disqualifies needing cross-service/model behavior to reproduce.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision tokens/objects, test the routing function writes them only to `decisions` and not to narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in transcript appear in summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM decision extraction/placement behavior from transcript content, and stubs would mask omission/misplacement failures (LLM-DEP + deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow: behavior starts from transcript and checks one summarization outcome, not necessarily multi-agent chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Send real transcript samples with clearly stated decisions to the production summarization model and assert those decisions are present in the structured `decisions` section rather than only narrative text.

behaviors.description: For audio longer than 60 minutes, service splits into segments, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is multi-step orchestration over multiple agentic calls (per-segment transcription/summarization plus merge), with coherence depending on chained model outputs and state handoff (MULTI-STEP + LLM-DEP). Rejected System: real infra alone misses coherence/handoff failures caused by sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation flow, verify segment sequencing occurred, and validate final merged summary preserves cross-segment continuity, decisions, and action items without duplication/loss.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes automatically for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets full end-to-end pipeline with real input and no manual intervention, requiring multiple sequential agentic stages plus real system interactions (upload/delivery). Any lower level can pass while real chained execution fails.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real meeting file in a production-like environment: upload, transcribe via real STT API, summarize via real LLM, and assert successful structured output delivery without operator steps.
